title: Bulk pencils — back-to-school garage-as-studio prep
html:
TL;DR

  • Aug 24, 2019 todo: "Bulk pencils / Bulk gum / Back bling / Clean garage / -more whiteboards / -desk/tables / -posters / Books" — pre-senior-year prep list mixing classroom supplies, a Fortnite back-bling reference, and a garage-rebuild for a study/workspace.
  • "Clean garage / more whiteboards / desk/tables / posters" — the garage-as-build-space pattern Yahir keeps revisiting; the same idea later mutates into the home lab / dorm-setup phase in 2022.
  • "Bulk pencils / bulk gum" — buying-in-volume tic that prefigures the small margin resale muscle later used in Pokémon card flipping.
  • "Back bling" — sole Fortnite reference in 2019 corpus; Yahir is 17 and the gaming layer of his life still surfaces in todo-mixed lists.
  • One-week-before-school timing — places this with 2019 08 22 mandarin class swaps and 2019 08 26 dream of dads key in the late-August 2019 senior-year-runway cluster.

Key items

  • Bulk pencils, bulk gum (volume-buy)
  • Back bling (Fortnite cosmetic)
  • Garage cleanup: whiteboards, desk/tables, posters
  • Books

Why it matters in the graph

The note is the earliest dated articulation of "garage = workspace" — three of seven lines are about converting the garage into a build/study room with whiteboards and posters. This is the same impulse later expressed as the dorm-room home lab, the multi-monitor desk in Austin, and the "set up the room for thinking" recurrent move.

The "bulk pencils / bulk gum" line is also the earliest volume-buy reflex in the corpus — the same instinct that produces Pokémon card resale and the later bridge income gigs. Yahir's 17-year-old self already saw bulk pricing as a lever.
